threats.title: How Synthetic Identity Fraud is Coming for Machine Identities
threats.summary: Most people understand identity theft as an attacker stealing a real person's sensitive information and impersonating them. Synthetic identity fraud is much harder to catch. Instead of stealing a real identity, the attacker manufactures a new one, frankensteining together several real data points with fabricated ones to create a person who doesn't exist. threats.title: Attackers Weaponize GitHub Actions Runners to Target cPanel and WHM Servers
threats.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have shed light on a large-scale campaign that has turned compromised GitHub repositories into distributed attack infrastructure designed to target cPanel and WebHost Manager (WHM) instances. threats.title: Google Adds Selfie Video Recovery for Users Locked Out of Their Accounts
threats.summary: Google on Thursday announced a new way for users to sign-in to their accounts by letting them take a selfie video. The selfie for sign-in, per the tech giant, is another option on top of existing recovery methods to log in to an account, including an email address or a phone number. threats.title: Nine-Year-Old RefluXFS Linux Flaw Gives Local Users Root on Default RHEL Installs
threats.summary: RefluXFS, a new Linux kernel flaw disclosed on July 22 and tracked as CVE-2026-64600, lets an unprivileged local user overwrite root-owned files on an XFS filesystem and gain persistent root access.
